entries.description: Add BuildingState type.
Add buildingState to App component and pass to children.
Add Raw/Ready building toggle below geometry slider for Interiors and Exteriors.
Update generation prompt to include raw building construction instructions when buildingState is 'raw'.
Update '2D Sketched' floorplan style prompt to ensure straight lines, remove text, and improve furniture rendering with a clean pencil aesthetic.

diffs.replacement:
import { InteriorIcon, ExteriorIcon, MoodboardIcon, RenderIcon, KeepIcon, ChangeIcon, EmptyIcon, FloorplanIcon } from './components/icons';
import { Category, FurnitureOption, Style } from './types';

export const CATEGORIES: Category[] = [
  { id: 'Interiors', Icon: InteriorIcon },
  { id: 'Exteriors', Icon: ExteriorIcon },
  { id: 'Moodboard', Icon: MoodboardIcon, isPro: true },
  { id: 'AI Render', Icon: RenderIcon, isPro: true },
  { id: 'Floorplan', Icon: FloorplanIcon, isPro: true },
];

export const FURNITURE_OPTIONS: FurnitureOption[] = [
  { id: 'keep', label: 'Keep Furniture', prompt: 'keeping the existing furniture, ', Icon: KeepIcon },
  { id: 'change', label: 'Change Furniture', prompt: 'replacing the furniture with new, stylish pieces, ', Icon: ChangeIcon },
  { id: 'empty', label: 'Empty Room', prompt: 'From a given interior image, remove all furniture and accessories while keeping the room’s architecture perfectly the same — preserve walls, windows, doors, ceilings, and floors. Output a clean architectural shell with no interior objects or decoration.', Icon: EmptyIcon },
];

export const FLOORPLAN_STYLES: Style[] = [
    { id: 'fp-2d-sketch', name: '2D Sketched', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1580587771525-78b9dba3b91d?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Convert this floorplan into a clean, professional architectural pencil sketch on a pure white background. Ensure all structural lines are perfectly straight and crisp, correcting any wobbliness. Remove all text, dimensions, and annotations completely. Render furniture with high-quality, detailed pencil sketching techniques—stylish and recognizable but maintaining a hand-drawn aesthetic. Use a strictly monochrome palette (black, grey, white) with subtle pencil shading for depth. The final result should look like a refined, high-end design sketch with no clutter.' },
    { id: 'fp-2d-color', name: '2D Textured', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1505691938895-1cd10274435d?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Convert this into a high-quality 2D real estate marketing floor plan. Show realistic flooring materials (wood, tile, carpet). Indicate furniture placement with a clean, modern top-down look. Soft shadows for depth.' },
    { id: 'fp-3d-white', name: '3D Iso Clay', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1517544845501-bb78ccdad085?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Convert this floor plan into a clean white clay 3D isometric render. Monochromatic white style, soft ambient occlusion shadows. Clear volumetric walls and cut-away view. Minimalist architectural model aesthetic.' },
    { id: 'fp-3d-real', name: '3D Iso Real', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1497366216548-37526070297c?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Create a highly realistic 3D isometric floor plan render while strictly preserving the exact geometry, wall layout, and proportions of the original image. Do not alter the structure. Full photorealistic materials for floors and walls. Include detailed realistic furniture, lighting, and cast shadows. Top-down isometric perspective.' }
];

export const CONCEPTUAL_STYLES: Style[] = [
  { id: 'conceptual-night', name: 'Night Mode', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558036117-15db52779ea2?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'professional photograph of the interior, at night, with professional architectural lighting, photorealistic, 8k' },
  { id: 'conceptual-sketch', name: 'Black Marker Sketch', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1531835551805-16d864c8d311?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Convert the architectural image into a black-marker hand sketch on white background.Lines should have uneven pressure and variable thickness, simulating hand-drawn imperfection.Keep all geometry and composition identical to the original render — just stylize the lines.' },
  { id: 'conceptual-marker', name: 'Marker', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1534438327276-14e5300c3a48?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'architectural marker rendering of the interior, vibrant colors, artistic' },
  { id: 'conceptual-3d', name: '3D', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1503387762-592deb58ef4e?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'From the uploaded 2D architectural image or render, convert it into a clean 3D architectural visualization while preserving all proportions, geometry, and composition exactly as in the original.\nEmphasize realistic depth, volumetric shading, and material differentiation (walls, floors, glazing, roofs, landscape).\nLighting should be soft and directional, with subtle shadows defining the 3D structure.\nKeep the camera view, perspective, and scale unchanged — the goal is to translate the image into a refined, spatial 3D visual style without altering design elements or proportions.\nOutput a clean, presentation-ready 3D look suitable for architectural concept boards' },
  { id: 'conceptual-exploded', name: 'Exploded', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1511818966892-d7d671e672a2?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'From the uploaded architectural or product image, generate an exploded 3D view that reveals the composition and hierarchy of all components.\nEach element (walls, slabs, roofs, façade panels, or furniture pieces) should be spatially separated along the vertical or diagonal axis, maintaining precise alignment and proportions relative to the original geometry.\nPreserve the exact design and materials — the goal is to visualize assembly, not redesign.\nUse soft lighting, neutral background, and thin shadow projection to accentuate depth and clarity.\nMaintain the same camera perspective and scale as the original.\nThe final image should look like a professional architectural or product axonometric exploded diagram, clean, balanced, and presentation-ready.' },
  { id: 'conceptual-watercolor', name: 'Watercolor Sketch', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1579783902614-a3fb3927b6a5?q=80&w=300&auto=format&fit=crop', prompt: 'Transform the villa image into a watercolor architectural sketch with light pastel tones, soft edges, and subtle washes of color. ];

payload.text: on the Styles add a button selection for "MOODBOARD" next to "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL"  : below this add a upload image of a moodboard to be used as reference style to stylish the interior or exterior.

**Adding Moodboard Selection**

I'm currently focused on incorporating a "Moodboard" option within the 'Styles' section of the sidebar. I'm aiming for it to sit alongside "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL," mirroring the existing structure, though adapting it for both Interior and Exterior designs, not just Exteriors. This feels like the right evolution for the styles panel.


**Refining Moodboard Integration**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating the "MOODBOARD" option into the sidebar's style selections. It looks like my first step is to modify the `Sidebar.tsx` file, ensuring "MOODBOARD" gets its own button, akin to "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL" for Exteriors. It seems I should keep the logic within the Exteriors section, making this change simpler than initially anticipated. I am also making sure to accommodate both interior and exterior use cases.


**Expanding Moodboard Scope**

I'm now contemplating how to integrate the "MOODBOARD" option comprehensively. Considering "stylish the interior or exterior," I realize the current implementation's limited scope is an issue. My immediate plan is to add the "MOODBOARD" toggle alongside the existing "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL" options, as the prompt specifies, and ensure that the interior category benefits as well. I need to make some tweaks to the `Sidebar.tsx` file for this feature to work as desired.


**Prioritizing UI Implementation**

I'm now focusing on implementing the "MOODBOARD" option and am prioritizing aligning it with the existing exterior UI, as the prompt directs. I think that since the UI directs this functionality next to 'PRE-SET' and 'CONCEPTUAL' that I should initially only target the exterior section. I'm considering adding it to the interior section later, if the codebase can be modified without significant changes. The aim is to quickly integrate the functionality while keeping the changes clean and localized.


**Aligning Exterior/Interior Moodboards**

I've realized the existing structure isn't entirely symmetrical. While I'm tasked with adding "MOODBOARD" alongside "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL" in the 'Styles' section for Exteriors, interiors have a different configuration. My current thinking is to modify the interior section too, by adding the "MOODBOARD" toggle where appropriate. It's a bit inconsistent that it doesn't currently have "PRE-SET" and "CONCEPTUAL", but I can change that. My focus, for now, is matching exterior and interior selections.


**Fixing Image Display**

I'm now prioritizing the image display issues. I've located `constants.ts` and am examining `ART_STYLES`. It seems the provided Unsplash URLs are the source of the problem. I'll replace those URLs with more reliable Unsplash image IDs. I'll focus on replacing the image URLs for "Coast," "Shabby Chic," "Tulum," and "Bali" to ensure their consistent display, and then check HD Photo renders.


**Validating Image URLs**

I am now focusing on troubleshooting the image display issue. I've re-examined the code. I think the issue is with the Unsplash URLs provided; I think they're broken. I intend to replace these URLs with new ones. I'll search for valid image URLs for the styles "Coast," "Shabby Chic," "Tulum," and "Bali," along with the "HD Photo" option, to ensure the consistent rendering of these styles.

I will also update `types.ts` to allow a new value for `ExteriorStyleSubCategory` or `StyleSubCategory` and I will modify `App.tsx` so `styleReferenceImage` and `styleReferenceImageUrl` work properly.


**Refining Moodboard Logic**

I'm now refining the logic related to the "MOODBOARD" selection, taking into account the nuances of both exterior and interior styles. I'm focusing on the interaction between `Sidebar.tsx`, the `generateImage` function, and how the `styleReferenceImage` is handled. I need to handle cases where `selectedStyle` might be unset. I'll focus on how the image is passed into the `generateImage` process.


**Adapting Prompt Generation**

I'm now grappling with how to generate prompts when the "Moodboard" option is selected. I realize the current system, particularly within `ImageEditor.tsx`, relies heavily on a `selectedStyle` object, which might be absent in Moodboard mode. My initial plan is to determine what the user uploads in `ImageEditor`. I'll need to modify the `ImageEditor` to accommodate the presence of a `styleReferenceImage`, even when `selectedStyle` is null. I'm exploring two options: either creating a dummy style, or modifying the logic to bypass the need for a pre-defined `selectedStyle` when a reference image is present. I am strongly leaning towards treating the uploaded moodboard image as the style reference for image generation. To adapt prompt generation, I need to make the generated prompt generic, such as "in the style of the reference image," or allow user input.


**Implementing Moodboard Prompt**

I'm now implementing the moodboard prompt. I've realized I need to modify `ImageEditor.tsx` to handle the scenario where a `styleReferenceImage` is available, but the `selectedStyle` is null, which can happen with Moodboard mode. I'm choosing to adapt the UI so that no style selection is required when in moodboard mode. I'll modify the prompt generation logic to incorporate the user's uploaded image. I'll change the prompt so it generically references the uploaded image as the style, such as "Render this in the style of the attached reference image". My next step is ensuring the image upload functionality works correctly.


**Adapting Prompt Generation**

I'm now contemplating prompt generation, particularly in Moodboard mode. It seems that if a `styleReferenceImage` exists, I need a baseline prompt, such as "finish this building". If the mode is styles and the subcategory is 'moodboard', a baseline prompt is still necessary. I'm considering defaulting the prompt to "Apply the style of the reference image to the input image" if no style is selected. I need to handle cases where there is no existing style selected, but a style reference image. I'll need to modify `geminiService` to accommodate those cases.


**Refining Prompt Generation**

I'm now zeroing in on prompt generation for moodboard use cases. My focus is on modifying the `handleGenerate` function within `ImageEditor.tsx`. I'll allow image generation even when `selectedStyle` is null, specifically when `styleReferenceImage` is available. I'll construct a default prompt that incorporates the user's uploaded image. I intend to default to a prompt like "Apply the style of the reference image to the input image" if no specific style is selected, and will test those cases within `ImageEditor`. I'm now testing these cases in isolation.


**Updating Category State**

I'm now focusing on generalizing the code to handle both interior and exterior styles. I'm modifying `types.ts` to rename `ExteriorStyleSubCategory` to `StyleSubCategory` and to add the `moodboard` option. In `App.tsx`, I'm renaming `exteriorStyleSubCategory` to `styleSubCategory`, and initializing it as `'preset'`. I will then modify the `Sidebar.tsx` file for the interior and exterior use cases.



**Generalizing Sidebar Logic**

I'm now generalizing the `Sidebar`'s behavior to handle both interior and exterior styles more consistently. I've updated `types.ts` to rename `ExteriorStyleSubCategory` to `StyleSubCategory` and added the `'moodboard'` option. In `App.tsx`, I've updated the state variable and its setter and passed them into the `Sidebar`. Now I will modify `Sidebar.tsx` to integrate the updates. I will then review the image URLs in `constants.ts`.


**Clarifying State Transitions**

I'm now clarifying how `selectedStyle` interacts with "Moodboard" mode. I'm focusing on ensuring `selectedStyle` becomes null when the Moodboard tab is selected in the Sidebar. I'll modify `Sidebar.tsx` to call `onApplyStyle(null)` when the Moodboard tab is clicked. This prevents visual confusion in the UI. Also, I must ensure `styleSubCategory` is passed to `ImageEditor`, so prompt generation can be modified.

To implement the requested changes, I will update the application to support a "Moodboard" style reference mode for both Interiors and Exteriors, fix the broken style images, and ensure the image editor handles the new moodboard generation logic correctly.

Here are the specific updates:

1.  **`types.ts`**: generalize `ExteriorStyleSubCategory` to `StyleSubCategory` and add `'moodboard'`.
2.  **`constants.ts`**: Update the broken URLs for Coastal, Shabby Chic, Tulum, Bali, and HD Photo styles.
3.  **`App.tsx`**: Update state management to use the new `StyleSubCategory` type and pass it to `ImageEditor`.
4.  **`components/Sidebar.tsx`**: Add the "MOODBOARD" toggle button to the Styles section (for both Interiors and Exteriors) and implement the moodboard upload UI.
5.  **`components/ImageEditor.tsx`**: Update the generation logic to allow processing without a selected style if a moodboard reference image is provided.

entries.description: Rename ExteriorStyleSubCategory to StyleSubCategory and add 'moodboard' option.
Update broken image URLs for specific styles.

**Refining Prompt Generation**

I've been focusing on refining the prompt generation aspect when the "MOODBOARD" option is selected. My current task involves integrating the uploaded moodboard to generate a more detailed prompt. I'm aiming to make it very relevant for image generation.


**Enhancing Moodboard Integration**

I'm now revising the prompt generation to deeply incorporate moodboard details, focusing on realism and material replication. I'm updating the `ImageEditor.tsx` component to make the process more explicit. The goal is to accurately translate the moodboard's aesthetic into the rendered image, avoiding any inconsistencies. The revised prompt structure will first acknowledge the input geometry and then the moodboard's stylistic influence.


**Implementing Detailed Specifications**

I am now focusing on detailed specifications to be included in the prompt. I will include instructions for material application that comes from the moodboard: including textures, color palettes, and furniture styles. I plan to incorporate these elements into the final image. I am including a section on furniture arrangement, selecting pieces based on the moodboard's style, and their arrangement. Finally, I will ensure high-fidelity surface application of the finishes from the moodboard, and photorealistic output with quality control measures.


**Defining Material Integration**

I'm now focusing on a more granular level of integration. I'm extracting specific material textures, color palettes, and furniture design from the moodboard. Then, I plan to apply these details to retexture the input geometry. I'll make sure flooring, walls, and ceiling match the moodboard's finishes. I'll also replace furniture with matching pieces, arranging them realistically. Finally, I'll aim for an 8k hyper-realistic visualization, with perfect perspective and natural lighting, ensuring no artifacts. I'll target a high-quality, photorealistic output.


**Integrating Furniture Preferences**

I am now integrating furniture preferences seamlessly into the moodboard process. If the user selects to 'keep' existing furniture, the prompt states this preference. Otherwise, I'm generating a style-matched replacement. I'm ensuring all moodboard instructions work well with these furniture selections. I've updated the `finalPrompt` construction to include these new details, improving the prompt's quality.


**Incorporating Lighting and Quality**

I'm now integrating lighting and quality control specifications into the prompt generation process. I need to replicate the lighting mood, whether warm, cool, soft, or dramatic, from the reference image. The prompt will also request a hyper-realistic, 8K architectural render. I'll ensure physical accuracy, correct scale, sharp details, and natural shadows, with no artifacts, distortions, or cartoonish looks. I am updating `components/ImageEditor.tsx`.


**Updating Furniture Styling**

The goal is to ensure a cohesive flow of information between the component and the overall application, ensuring that the image generation process responds correctly to changes in the external states. I've included `handleImageReset` to clear image states after upload, including the generated image URL, any errors, and the comparison state. I'm also addressing drag-and-drop functionality with the `handleDrop` event.


**Constructing the Final Prompt**

I'm now integrating the `buildingInstruction` and `furniturePrompt` variables into the final prompt for moodboard-driven image generation. My process involves validating the style reference image to guarantee it's available. The `finalPrompt` will instruct the image generation to complete construction, including finishes, tiling, landscaping, and furniture styling. This aims to create a fully finished image based on the input moodboard.


**Defining Aesthetic Fidelity**

I am now focusing on accurately transferring the aesthetic elements from the moodboard. I'll meticulously replicate wood tones, stone textures, fabrics, paint colors, and metal finishes to the walls, floors, and surfaces of the input room. I plan to incorporate furniture that aligns with the era and style seen in the moodboard, and arrange it realistically. Finally, I'm aiming for the same lighting mood and a hyper-realistic 8K architectural render without any artifacts or distortions.


**Prioritizing Style Transfer**

I'm now zeroing in on accurate style replication from the moodboard. I'm focusing on materials, furniture style, and lighting. I'm refining the process to guarantee consistent transfer, with a priority on photorealistic results. I'm focusing on the `finalPrompt` construction in the `ImageEditor.tsx` component to make sure details align to the output.


**Evaluating Image Display**

I'm now evaluating how images display in `ImageEditor.tsx`. I'm working with the `ResultUrl` logic to show either the original or generated image based on the context. If the image is loading, a loading state appears. If no image exists, I'll show a placeholder. I've ensured a smooth transition between these states.


**Refining Comparison Display**

I'm now revising how images are shown within `ImageEditor.tsx`. I've integrated a comparison slider, which has custom handle controls to compare the original and generated images. I have also refined the logic to ensure that the images load correctly based on context. I'm focusing on providing smooth transitions and visual feedback during loading states.


**Defining User Mode Feedback**

I'm now integrating feedback for user modes within the `ImageEditor.tsx` component. The system will now clearly indicate whether moodboard or prompt-based rendering is selected. The messages update with the mode choice. I'll make sure it's intuitive.


**Enhancing Drag-and-Drop**

I'm focusing on ensuring this slider interacts seamlessly with the image rendering process and the moodboard instructions, leading to improved image quality. The slider's value will update the prompt sent to the AI, ensuring a wider range of control over final image appearance.

I'm focusing on managing the display of the loading spinner, the original image, and the generated image depending on the loading states and data availability. **Optimizing Download and Expand**

I've added logic to disable the "Download" and "Expand" buttons when images are not available, improving the user experience. I've also integrated functionality to enable these buttons once images become accessible. I'm prioritizing the overall UX within `ImageEditor.tsx`.

– floor materials
– ceiling materials and details
– furniture materials and finishes
– pool finishes and tiles (if present)

– Maintain existing furniture positions and scale.
– Change only materials, upholstery, finishes, and colors to match the moodboard.
– Accessories (curtains, cushions, decor objects, small loose furniture) may be adjusted, replaced, or simplified only if necessary to align with the moodboard style.

– Use the moodboard as the sole reference for textures, materials, colors, and overall design language.
– Match surface finishes, gloss/matte levels, wood grain direction, stone veining, fabric texture, and pool tile patterns accurately.
– Do not invent styles, colors, or materials not present in the moodboard.

Lighting & realism:
– Create a high-end, editorial-quality architectural image.
– Use soft, realistic lighting with natural shadows and balanced contrast.
– Maintain a refined, luxury atmosphere suitable for an architectural presentation or client pitch.
– Improve material realism, reflections, depth, and texture fidelity without altering the design.

Output quality:
– Ultra-realistic, professional architectural visualization

import { InteriorIcon, ExteriorIcon, MoodboardIcon, RenderIcon, KeepIcon, ChangeIcon, EmptyIcon, FloorplanIcon } from './components/icons';
import { Category, FurnitureOption, Style } from './types';

export const CATEGORIES: Category[] = [
  { id: 'Interiors', Icon: InteriorIcon },
  { id: 'Exteriors', Icon: ExteriorIcon },
  { id: 'Moodboard', Icon: MoodboardIcon, isPro: true },
  { id: 'AI Render', Icon: RenderIcon, isPro: true },
  { id: 'Floorplan', Icon: FloorplanIcon, isPro: true },
];

export const FURNITURE_OPTIONS: FurnitureOption[] = [
  { id: 'keep', label: 'Keep Furniture', prompt: 'keeping the existing furniture, ', Icon: KeepIcon },
  { id: 'change', label: 'Change Furniture', prompt: 'replacing the furniture with new, stylish pieces, ', Icon: ChangeIcon },
  { id: 'empty', label: 'Empty Room', prompt: 'From a given interior image, remove all furniture and accessories while keeping the room’s architecture perfectly the same — preserve walls, windows, doors, ceilings, and floors. Output a clean architectural shell with no interior objects or decoration.', Icon: EmptyIcon },
];

export const FLOORPLAN_STYLES: Style[] = [
    { id: 'fp-2d-sketch', name: '2D Sketched', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1596522354195-e8448ea16a92?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Convert this floorplan into a clean, professional architectural pencil sketch on a pure white background. Ensure all structural lines are perfectly straight and crisp, correcting any wobbliness. Remove all text, dimensions, and annotations completely. Render furniture with high-quality, detailed pencil sketching techniques—stylish and recognizable but maintaining a hand-drawn aesthetic. Use a strictly monochrome palette (black, grey, white) with subtle pencil shading for depth. The final result should look like a refined, high-end design sketch with no clutter.' },
    { id: 'fp-2d-color', name: '2D Textured', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1558036117-15db52779ea2?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Convert this into a high-quality 2D real estate marketing floor plan. Show realistic flooring materials (wood, tile, carpet). Indicate furniture placement with a clean, modern top-down look. Soft shadows for depth.' },
    { id: 'fp-3d-white', name: '3D Iso Clay', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1517544845501-bb78ccdad085?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Convert this floor plan into a clean white clay 3D isometric render. Monochromatic white style, soft ambient occlusion shadows. Clear volumetric walls and cut-away view. Minimalist architectural model aesthetic.' },
    { id: 'fp-3d-real', name: '3D Iso Real', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1592305596323-95b060613867?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Create a highly realistic 3D isometric floor plan render while strictly preserving the exact geometry, wall layout, and proportions of the original image. Do not alter the structure. Full photorealistic materials for floors and walls. Include detailed realistic furniture, lighting, and cast shadows. Top-down isometric perspective.' }
];

export const CONCEPTUAL_STYLES: Style[] = [
  { id: 'conceptual-night', name: 'Night Mode', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1516455590571-18256e5bb9ff?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'professional photograph of the interior, at night, with professional architectural lighting, photorealistic, 8k' },
  { id: 'conceptual-sketch', name: 'Black Marker Sketch', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1544161515-4ab6ce6db874?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Convert the architectural image into a black-marker hand sketch on white background.Lines should have uneven pressure and variable thickness, simulating hand-drawn imperfection.Keep all geometry and composition identical to the original render — just stylize the lines.' },
  { id: 'conceptual-marker', name: 'Marker', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1574739596292-b429e4b753f5?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'architectural marker rendering of the interior, vibrant colors, artistic' },
  { id: 'conceptual-3d', name: '3D', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600607687939-ce8a6c25118c?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'From the uploaded 2D architectural image or render, convert it into a clean 3D architectural visualization while preserving all proportions, geometry, and composition exactly as in the original.\nEmphasize realistic depth, volumetric shading, and material differentiation (walls, floors, glazing, roofs, landscape).\nLighting should be soft and directional, with subtle shadows defining the 3D structure.\nKeep the camera view, perspective, and scale unchanged — the goal is to translate the image into a refined, spatial 3D visual style without altering design elements or proportions.\nOutput a clean, presentation-ready 3D look suitable for architectural concept boards' },
  { id: 'conceptual-exploded', name: 'Exploded', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1503387762-592deb58ef4e?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'From the uploaded architectural or product image, generate an exploded 3D view that reveals the composition and hierarchy of all components.\nEach element (walls, slabs, roofs, façade panels, or furniture pieces) should be spatially separated along the vertical or diagonal axis, maintaining precise alignment and proportions relative to the original geometry.\nPreserve the exact design and materials — the goal is to visualize assembly, not redesign.\nUse soft lighting, neutral background, and thin shadow projection to accentuate depth and clarity.\nMaintain the same camera perspective and scale as the original.\nThe final image should look like a professional architectural or product axonometric exploded diagram, clean, balanced, and presentation-ready.' },
  { id: 'conceptual-watercolor', name: 'Watercolor Sketch', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1582268611958-ebfd161ef9cf?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Transform the villa image into a watercolor architectural sketch with light pastel tones, soft edges, and subtle washes of color. Preserve the composition and proportions exactly. The effect should look hand-painted, delicate, and presentation-ready.' },
];

export const EXTERIOR_RENDER_STYLES: Style[] = [
  { id: 'render-night', name: 'Night Mode', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600607686527-6fb886090705?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'professional photograph, exterior, night, warm architectural lighting, photorealistic, 8k' },
  { id: 'render-hd', name: 'HD Photo', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600596542815-6ad4c7213299?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'Enhance the realism and photographic quality of the image while keeping the geometry, proportions, and composition exactly the same.\nConvert the render into a natural, high-end architectural photograph with balanced lighting, soft shadows, and realistic depth.\nUse warm and natural light conditions (such as late afternoon or golden hour) to add atmosphere and depth withoutchanging the design.\nImprove textures, reflections, vegetation, and sky realism — but do not alter the shapes, layout, or architectural elements.\nPreserve the original camera angle and spatial arrangement.\nAim for a cinematic, ultra-realistic, professional result — natural lighting, true-to-life details, and refined ambience.' },
  { id: 'render-snow', name: 'Snow', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1483323324317-5735b5a2542a?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'professional photograph, exterior, covered in a fresh layer of snow, winter scene, crisp lighting, photorealistic, 8k' },
  { id: 'render-3d', name: '3D Style', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600585154526-990dced4db0d?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'From the uploaded 2D architectural image or render, convert it into a clean 3D architectural visualization while preserving all proportions, geometry, and composition exactly as in the original.\nEmphasize realistic depth, volumetric shading, and material differentiation (walls, floors, glazing, roofs, landscape).\nLighting should be soft and directional, with subtle shadows defining the 3D structure.\nKeep the camera view, perspective, and scale unchanged — the goal is to translate the image into a refined, spatial 3D visual style without altering design elements or proportions.\nOutput a clean, presentation-ready 3D look suitable for architectural concept boards' },
  { id: 'render-architecture', name: 'Architecture', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1486744360538-e25faaef0213?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'architectural blueprint drawing of the exterior, clean lines, monochrome, technical diagram' },
];


export const ART_STYLES: Style[] = [
    { id: 'art-minimalist', name: 'Minimalist', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1598928506311-c55ded91a20c?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A minimalist interior with clean lines, a neutral color palette, and uncluttered spaces.' },
    { id: 'art-modern', name: 'Modern', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1618221195710-dd6b41faaea6?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A modern interior characterized by simplicity, natural materials, and a connection to the outdoors.' },
    { id: 'art-contemporary', name: 'Contemporary', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1616486338812-3dadae4b4ace?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: `A contemporary interior design style, characterized by its fluid, curved lines, and a sophisticated, upscale feel.` },
    { id: 'art-industrial', name: 'Industrial', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1615529182904-14819c35db37?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'An industrial-style interior featuring exposed brick walls, metal accents, and a raw, unfinished aesthetic.' },
    { id: 'art-scandinavian', name: 'Scandinavian', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1595515106969-1ce29566ff1c?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A Scandinavian-inspired interior with light wood floors, white walls, and cozy, functional furniture.' },
    { id: 'art-rustic', name: 'Rustic', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1616047006789-b7af5afb8c20?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A rustic interior with natural wood beams, stone elements, and a warm, inviting atmosphere.' },
    { id: 'art-coastal', name: 'Coastal', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1595558496464-9d57a4199c9c?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A coastal or Hamptons-style interior with light, airy spaces, natural textures, and a blue and white color scheme.' },
    { id: 'art-deco', name: 'Art Deco', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1617103996702-96ff29b1c467?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'An Art Deco-inspired interior with bold geometric patterns, luxurious materials like gold and marble, and a glamorous feel.' },
    { id: 'art-mid-century', name: 'Mid-Century Modern', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1567225557594-88d73e55f2cb?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A Mid-Century Modern interior with iconic furniture pieces, organic shapes, and a seamless flow between indoors and outdoors.' },
    { id: 'art-farmhouse', name: 'Farmhouse', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1560448204-603b3fc33ddc?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A modern farmhouse interior with shiplap walls, a neutral palette, and comfortable, practical furnishings.' },
    { id: 'art-japanese-zen', name: 'Japanese Zen', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1583847268964-b28dc8f51f92?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A Japanese Zen interior that emphasizes simplicity, natural elements, and a sense of tranquility.' },
    { id: 'art-mediterranean', name: 'Mediterranean', image: 'https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1600210491892-03d54c0aaf87?auto=format&fit=crop&w=300&q=80', prompt: 'A Mediterranean interior with terracotta tiles, arched doorways, and a warm, earthy color palette.' },
